comments.text: the $m(n)\to \infty$ part is extraneous. this is due to a supersaturation argument and a "weak hypergraph Nikiforov" argument, similar to in #161 (specifically the partial result by Conlon, Fox, and Sudakov).

so, define $\mathcal{F}_r$ to be the family of $r$-graphs obtained by starting with $K_{r,\dots,r}^{(r)}$ and adding one more hyperedge (crucially we must consider the entire family, since blowing up any $F\in \mathcal{F}_r$ will give a $((1/r)^r+\Omega(1))$-dense hypergraph which does not contain the other $F'\in \mathcal{F}_r\setminus \{F\}$). it would suffice to show $\text{ex}(n,\mathcal{F}_r)= ((1/r)^r+o(1))n^r$ (and this presumably is true). unfortunately I am not the most well-versed in extremal hypergraph stuff. perhaps somebody can finish it from here? (I tried to ask GPT 5 Thinking and Gemini 2.5 Pro about the $r=3$ case. but after several failures, I gave up.)
